summary: Request to make SKNativeObject.IsDisposed public so external callers can check disposal state of SKBitmap and other SkiaSharp objects without reflection or exception-based workarounds.

stepsToReproduce: Try to access skBitmap.IsDisposed from user code outside SkiaSharp assembly
Observe that IsDisposed is protected internal and inaccessible

analysis.summary: The IsDisposed property is declared as `protected internal` on SKNativeObject, the abstract base class for all SkiaSharp objects. Changing it to `public` is a straightforward, ABI-safe additive change that would expose disposal state to all consumers.
analysis.rationale: The property already exists in SKNativeObject; the request is simply to widen its visibility from `protected internal` to `public`. This is an enhancement to existing functionality rather than a new feature. The fix path is clear and the change is ABI-safe (additive). Multiple commenters have confirmed the need, and the workarounds (reflection, try/catch, Handle == IntPtr.Zero check) are all fragile. The race-condition caveat noted in comments is valid but is the consumer's responsibility — not a reason to withhold the API.

keySignals.text: it sure would be nice if I could do a safety check with "skbitmap.IsDisposed" -- but I cannot, because the getter is Protected, instead of Public.
keySignals.interpretation: Direct request to change access modifier on an existing property.

keySignals.text: reflection is not a reliable option for commercial software that needs to be either obfuscated or machine compiled
keySignals.interpretation: Existing workaround is not viable for production use — confirms the need for a public API.

keySignals.text: You can try to test `if (skobject.Handle == IntPtr.Zero)` but the race condition still exists.
keySignals.interpretation: Even the Handle check has the same race limitation; exposing IsDisposed does not introduce new problems.

codeInvestigation.finding: `protected internal bool IsDisposed => isDisposed == 1;` is defined on SKNativeObject. Changing to `public` requires modifying only this one line and affects all derived objects (SKBitmap, SKPath, SKCanvas, etc.).
SKNativeObject is the root IDisposable base. Its Handle property is already `public virtual`, and Dispose() is public. Making IsDisposed public is consistent with the existing public surface.
IsDisposed is already used extensively in internal HandleDictionary code — confirming the property is stable and its semantics are well-understood.

workarounds: Use reflection to invoke protected getter: `(bool)typeof(SKObject).GetProperty("IsDisposed", BindingFlags.NonPublic|BindingFlags.Instance).GetValue(obj)`
Check `skObject.Handle == IntPtr.Zero` as a disposal proxy (not perfectly reliable in all cases)
Wrap access in a try/catch on property access to detect use-after-dispose
nextQuestions: Should `OwnsHandle` and `IgnorePublicDispose` also be made public for parity, or is IsDisposed sufficient?

resolution.hypothesis: Change access modifier of `IsDisposed` from `protected internal` to `public` on `SKNativeObject` (line 242 of SKObject.cs). This is an additive, ABI-safe change.

proposals.title: Make IsDisposed public on SKNativeObject
proposals.description: Change `protected internal bool IsDisposed` to `public bool IsDisposed` in SKNativeObject. All SkiaSharp objects inherit this base class so all consumers benefit immediately.

resolution.recommendedReason: Single-line change, ABI-safe, affects all objects, consistent with the already-public Handle and Dispose() members.

actionability.reason: Root cause is clear, fix path is a single-line access modifier change in SKNativeObject, no ABI risk.

actions.description: Correct label from feature-request to enhancement and add area/SkiaSharp and tenet/reliability
Confirm the enhancement is well-scoped, identify the single-line fix location, and note the race-condition caveat.
